meaning: adj.易见的,显著的,突出的,跳跃的n.凸角,突出部分
examples: In many cases, goals have economic rewards that make them more salient or powerful.
Economists label the problem "present bias": we are vulnerable to fast, salient stimulation.
Before colonialism, gender was more salient in central Kenya than it was in Ghana, although age was still crucial in determining authority.
The more salient environmental issues become, they argue, the better the Lib Dems should do.
Finally, the other salient increase in the rate of inflation occurred between 1986 and 1990.
